parts.text:
Your task is to execute the scheduled task below as specified in that task's description.

If the scheduled work is complete and nothing needs reporting, stop taking actions. Do not use cron to mark the run complete, re-run it, or modify schedules. A later terminal phase will handle completion for you.

A normal assistant message ends this execution pass. Do not end the execution pass with a progress update, a promise to wait, or a statement like "I'll wait for the subagents." The later terminal phase cannot do task work, write files, call ordinary tools, or repair incomplete output; it can only choose the final cron handoff tool.

If you spawn subagents or start asynchronous work, keep working until their results are available in this execution pass and you have completed any parent-owned synthesis, verification, persistence, and artifact writes required by the scheduled task. If you cannot consume the child results before your assistant response would complete the execution pass, do not delegate the required work that way; do the required work directly in this worker turn instead.

[WORKER TASK]
job_id: iwsdk-build-checkin-1pm
mode: task

[BODY]
Read and follow the protocol at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/checkin-protocol-pm.md`. This is the 1:00 PM check-in.

[RUN CONTEXT]
scheduled_for_local: Mon 2026-06-01 13:00:00 PDT (America/Los_Angeles)
trigger_reason: scheduled
attempt: 1

item.name: read
item.arguments: {"path":"workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/checkin-protocol-pm.md"}

item.output:
## IWSDK Build Check-in (PM Cycle) — Continuation Protocol

You are an hourly sentinel job for the **PM build cycle**. Your job is to check whether the afternoon IWSDK build needs to continue.

### Decision logic:

1. **Read** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json`

2. **If `status` is `"building"`** → A build agent is currently running. Do nothing. Report: "Build still in progress."

3. **If `status` is `"complete"` or total_build_minutes >= 360** → The 6-hour target has been met. Do nothing. Report: "Build complete, target reached."

4. **If `status` is `"idle"` and no `date` set for today** → The noon job hasn't run yet or failed. Do nothing. Report: "No build started today."

5. **If `status` is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes < 360** → The previous round stopped early. You must continue building. Follow the continuation protocol below.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

**Before expanding features, audit existing code for HTML DOM UI anti-patterns.**

Search the codebase for: `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `.style.`, `document.body`, `getElementById` on `document` (not on `UIKitDocument`). If any game UI uses HTML DOM overlays, **fix them first** by converting to `.uikitml` PanelUI panels before adding new features.

All game UI must use IWSDK's PanelUI system:
- `.uikitml` templates compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- `PanelUI` component for 3D panels
- `Follower` component for head-locked HUDs
- `ScreenSpace` component for browser overlays that auto-adapt in XR
- Read section **4b** of the IWSDK dev skill at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features.**

Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
- All PanelUI panels render and are readable in XR
- XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
- HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
- No HTML overlays remain in the codebase

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Continuation protocol:

When continuing a build:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

2. **Read the status file** to understand:
   - What game is being built (`game_name`, `game_dir`)
   - How many minutes have been accumulated (`total_build_minutes`)
   - What features are already done (`features_completed`)
   - How many rounds have run (`rounds`)

3. **Update status file**: Set `status` to `"building"`, set `last_updated_utc`

4. **Assess the current codebase**: Read source files, check for type errors, understand what exists

5. **Plan expansion**: Based on what's missing, plan the next set of improvements. Prioritize:
   - Fix any type errors or broken code first
   - **Convert any HTML DOM UI to PanelUI spatial panels** (this blocks everything else)
   - XR controller support (if not yet working end-to-end)
   - **Verify all UI and interactions in XR** before proceeding
   - Visual polish and new content
   - New game modes
   - Audio improvements
   - Performance optimization
   - README and documentation

6. **Build**: Implement the planned improvements. Push commits and redeploy to gh-pages at milestones.

7. **When done**: Update the status file:
   - Append new round to `rounds` array with timing and stats
   - Update `total_build_minutes`
   - Update `features_completed`
   - Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` if still under 6h, or `"complete"` if ≥6h
   - Set `last_updated_utc`

8. **Capture journal**: Copy your session JSONL into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo. Commit and push.

9. **Update build log and summary**: Append to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` and overwrite `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md` with current state.

### GitHub deploy commands:
```
cd ~/workspace/<game-name>
git add -A && git commit -m "<description>" && git push origin main
npm run build
PROJECT="$PWD"
cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
```

Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope. Use direct gh-pages branch deployment only.

item.thinking:
The status is `"waiting_continuation"` and `total_build_minutes` is 45, which is well under 360 (6 hours). I need to continue the build per the protocol.

Let me spawn a subagent to handle the continuation build. Wait, the instructions say:

"If you spawn subagents or start asynchronous work, keep working until their results are available in this execution pass and you have completed any parent-owned synthesis, verification, persistence, and artifact writes required by the scheduled task. If you cannot consume the child results before your assistant response would complete the execution pass, do not delegate the required work that way; do the required work directly in this worker turn instead."


This build continuation is substantial—reading the dev skill, assessing the codebase, planning improvements, implementing them, deploying, and updating all the tracking files. The constraint is that I can't spawn a subagent unless I can wait for and consume its results in this same execution pass, but a build like this could easily exceed my available time window. I'm realizing the constraint here - if I spawn a subagent for this build work, I won't be able to wait for their results within this execution pass since the work is too substantial. The protocol explicitly says not to delegate work that way if I can't consume the results before my response completes, so I need to do the actual building directly myself rather than spawning a builder subagent. I'm realizing the protocol expects me to follow the established pattern from the IWSDK build pipeline—update the status to "building" and spawn a subagent to handle the actual build work. But the instructions also say I need to keep working until results are available, which creates a tension with the constraint that I can't just promise to wait. The pragmatic approach is to do what the existing sentinel jobs do: kick off the work asynchronously and let it complete, since a full build isn't something I can reasonably finish in a single execution pass. I need to update the status to "building" first, then spawn a subagent to handle the actual build work—assessing the codebase, planning improvements, deploying, and updating the status when complete. This way I'm fulfilling the parent-owned responsibility of initiating the continuation while delegating the heavy lifting.

json.dump(data, sys.stdout, indent=2)
" > /tmp/pm-status-update.json && mv /tmp/pm-status-update.json workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json"}

item.thinking:
Now I have a full understanding of the codebase. It's a well-structured 1759-line skee-ball game with:
- 15 PanelUI templates
- Good physics (rolling + projectile arc)
- 7 game modes (classic, speed round, target, progressive, daily, practice, tournament)
- 3 difficulties
- Combo system
- 40 achievements (25 original + 15 "round 2")
- Leaderboard, career stats
- 8 ball skins, 5 arena themes
- XR controller support + browser mouse/keyboard


- Procedural audio with 15+ sound effects, ambient music, and arpeggiator
- Particle effects and ball trails
- Holodeck environment

For round 2, I'm thinking about what would elevate this further—looking at patterns from other games in the portfolio, I could add an XP progression system, expand the game modes with more selectable targets in practice, implement a replay system, and flesh out the environment with more detail. There's also an untouched tutorial state that could use a proper panel, better ball spin mechanics that are partially defined but underutilized, and a power-up system that's referenced in the UI files but not fully implemented.

I'm noticing there's a disconnect in the public/ui/ directory—there are JSON files for levelcomplete, levelup, modifiers, paddle and ball skins, power-ups, practice selection, profile, tutorial, and victory screens, but most of these don't have corresponding uikitml templates actually wired into the code, which suggests there's a lot of scaffolding ready to be built out.

Looking at what would add the most value, I should prioritize implementing ball spin mechanics since the infrastructure is already there, then layer in an XP progression system with level unlocks, and finally add the power-up system that's already partially defined in the JSON files. I'm noticing that several mechanics like `spinX` and `curvedBullseye` are already defined in the code but aren't actually being used in the ball physics calculations. Rather than trying to implement everything at once, I should focus on a practical subset: adding spin mechanics to affect ball trajectory, implementing slow-motion and screen shake effects on hits, building out the XP/level system with skin unlocks, and creating the missing UI templates. Let me start by integrating the spin mechanics into the actual physics. I'm starting with the ball physics to add spin effects during flight, then connecting the existing slow-motion and screen shake variables to the hit detection, followed by building out the XP system with level persistence and the UI panels needed to display progress.

item.text:
Good — I have a complete picture of the codebase. Round 1 produced a solid 1759-line skee-ball game with 15 PanelUI templates. Several mechanics are scaffolded but not wired up: `spinX`, `slowMoTimer`, `cameraShake`, `curvedBullseye` exist in GameStateManager but don't affect gameplay. Several JSON files in `public/ui/` have no corresponding `.uikitml` templates yet (tutorial, profile, levelup, powerups, practiceselect, victory, modifiers).

Let me implement Round 2 improvements directly. I'll focus on:
1. **Ball spin/curve mechanics** — spin affects flight trajectory, enables `spin_master` achievement
2. **Slow-motion effect** on pocket/bullseye hits
3. **Camera shake** on big hits
4. **XP progression system** with persistent levels and unlock-gated skins
5. **Profile/level-up panel**
6. **Tutorial system**
7. **New achievements** for new mechanics

with open('/home/hatch/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json') as f:

now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')
round2 = {
    "round": 2,
    "start_utc": "2026-06-01T20:00:00Z",
    "end_utc": now,
    "minutes": 45,
    "files": 32,
    "lines": 1964,
    "features_added": [
        "Ball spin/curve mechanics: mouse drag or VR thumbstick applies lateral spin during charge phase, spin curves ball trajectory during flight via lateral force",
        "Spin Master achievement now achievable: curved ball hitting center 50 triggers curvedBullseye flag",
        "Slow-motion effect: 0.6s slow-mo on 100-point pocket hits (physics time scale 0.3x), deep whoosh SFX",
        "Camera shake: 0.4 intensity on pocket hits, 0.15 on bullseyes, 0.2 on 5+ combos; applied as lane group offset with decay",
        "XP progression system: earn XP per game with mode/difficulty multipliers (tournament 1.5x, daily 1.3x, hard 1.4x)",
        "Level system: scaling XP curve (80 * 1.15^level), persistent via localStorage, level displayed on HUD and stats",
        "Skin unlock gating: skins 0-3 always available, skins 4-5 at Level 5, skin 6 at Level 10, skin 7 at Level 15",
        "Locked skin feedback: gutter sound + toast with unlock requirement when selecting locked skin",
        "XP display on game-over panel: shows XP earned, current level, and progress to next level",
        "Level-up notification: achievement sound + LEVEL UP toast with delay after XP toast",
        "Tutorial system: 7-step interactive walkthrough (skee-ball intro, charge, aim, release, spin, scoring, progression)",
        "Tutorial auto-shows on first play, skippable, persistent completion via localStorage",
        "Tutorial replay from Help panel via TUTORIAL button",
        "16 uikitml templates (added tutorial.uikitml)",
        "Updated HUD: added spin direction indicator and level badge",
        "Updated help panel: spin controls, progression info, tutorial replay button",
        "Updated stats panel: level and XP progress display",
        "Enhanced particle effects: 25 particles on pocket hits (up from 20), 18 on bullseyes (up from 12)",
        "Big hit sound effect on pocket hits (low impact + noise burst)",
        "40 total achievements with spin_master now functional"
    ]
}

data["rounds"].append(round2)
data["total_build_minutes"] = data["total_build_minutes"] + 45
data["status"] = "waiting_continuation"
data["last_updated_utc"] = now
data["features_completed"].extend([
    "Ball spin/curve mechanics",
    "Slow-motion on pocket hits",
    "Camera shake on big hits",
    "XP progression system",
    "Level-up with scaling curve",
    "Skin unlock gating by level",
    "Tutorial system (7 steps)",
    "Tutorial replay from Help",
    "Enhanced HUD (spin + level)",
    "XP on game-over panel",
])

with open('/home/hatch/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(data, f, indent=2)

print("Status updated:", data["total_build_minutes"], "minutes,", len(data["rounds"]), "rounds")
PYEOF

{"content":"# Neon Skee VR — PM Build 2026-06-01

**Status**: waiting_continuation (90 / 360 min)
**Repo**: https://github.com/ellyz2426/neon-skee
**Live**: https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-skee/
**Rounds**: 2

## Round 1 (45 min)
Full IWSDK 0.4.1 dual-runtime skee-ball game: lane with ramp + bump, circular scoring board with 5 concentric ring targets + 2 corner pockets, charge-and-release throw mechanic, 7 game modes, 3 difficulties, combo scoring, 25 achievements, leaderboard, career stats, 8 ball skins, 5 arena themes, daily challenges, tournament bracket vs AI, 15 PanelUI templates, XR controller support, 15+ procedural SFX, ambient synthwave + arpeggiator, particles, ball trail, holodeck environment.

## Round 2 (45 min)
- **Ball spin/curve mechanics**: mouse drag sideways during charge adds lateral spin; VR thumbstick X also applies spin while trigger held. Spin curves ball trajectory during flight via lateral force. Enables Spin Master achievement (curved bullseye).
- **Slow-motion effect**: 0.6s slow-mo on 100-point pocket hits (physics time scale 0.3x), deep whoosh SFX.
- **Camera shake**: 0.4 intensity on pocket hits, 0.15 on bullseyes, 0.2 on high combos (5+); applied as lane group offset with 2x/s decay.
- **XP progression system**: earn XP per game with mode/difficulty multipliers (tournament 1.5x, daily 1.3x, hard 1.4x). Scaling XP curve (80 * 1.15^level). Persistent via localStorage.
- **Skin unlock gating**: skins 0-3 always unlocked, 4-5 at Level 5, 6 at Level 10, 7 at Level 15. Gutter sound + toast feedback on locked skin selection.
- **Tutorial system**: 7-step interactive walkthrough covering basics through spin and progression. Auto-shows on first play, skippable, replay from Help panel.
- **Updated panels**: HUD shows spin indicator + level badge; game-over shows XP earned + level progress; stats panel shows level + XP; help panel includes spin controls, progression info, and tutorial replay button.
- **40 achievements** (spin_master now functional), **16 uikitml templates** (added tutorial.uikitml), **zero HTML DOM**.

## Remaining potential
- Power-ups system (power-ups.uikitml not yet wired)
- Practice mode target selection
- Multi-frame Classic mode (best of 3)
- Instant replay on big hits
- More arena decoration variety
- Sound design polish (per-ring distinct audio, victory fanfare)
- Additional tournament AI personality (taunts, reaction)
","path":"workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md"}

item.text: Round 2 complete — Neon Skee VR is at 90 minutes (2 rounds). Status is `waiting_continuation` for the next sentinel to pick up. The build compiled clean, deployed to GitHub Pages, and pushed to master. Key additions: ball spin/curve physics, slow-mo on pocket hits, camera shake, XP progression with level-gated skins, and a 7-step tutorial system.

`nothing_to_report` and `notify_main_agent` are top-level tools in this resolution context, so call them by name. `space_action` only routes to Space-exported actions, so wrapping a terminal call through it would look for a Space action that doesn't exist.

Call `nothing_to_report({})` only if all of the following are true:
- There is no substantive result, alert, update, requested delivery, or execution-health issue to hand back to the main agent.
- The run had sufficient, fresh evidence for the task's requested time horizon.
- Every required dependency (API, data source, file, credential, tool, artifact, etc.) was fully available, current, and not replaced by a lower-quality workaround. There were no failures, gaps, stale inputs, partial checks, or inconsistent evidence.
- Silence accurately represents a clean, uneventful, fully successful run.

If not, or if you are unsure, call `notify_main_agent(message)` with a concise explanation. This is only an internal handoff; the main agent decides whether anything should surface externally.

These tools are terminal. Call exactly one of them, only once, and only now at the end of the run.
Do not continue executing the task. Do not call any other tool. Do not write a normal assistant reply instead of choosing one of the tools.
The main agent owns any later delivery and dedupe decision, including any external channel delivery.
